fingerprint: Azimuthal symmetry breaking: differential decay between two cells at similar latitudes requires non-axisymmetric (m≠0) spherical harmonic terms that only exist in globe geometry. The dome's pole-anchored aetheric field is axisymmetric by construction and cannot produce different decay rates at the same radial distance. Additionally, the 21,795 nT prediction threshold (WIN-035) is derived from an exponential curve fitted to CHAOS-7 output — a circular prediction of globe-model extrapolation.
globe_value: In globe geophysics, differential SAA cell decay arises from non-dipole Gauss coefficients (g₂¹, h₂¹, g₃¹, h₃¹, etc.) — the m≠0 spherical harmonic terms that encode longitudinal asymmetry in Earth's core field. The African cell (~30°E, 25°S) and Atlantic cell (~50°W, 25°S) sit at similar colatitudes but different azimuths. Their different decay rates (African: ~160–200 nT/yr, Atlantic: ~80–120 nT/yr) arise from different secular variation of specific (n,m) mode contributions — particularly the g₂⁰ and g₃⁰ axial terms interacting with the g₂¹, h₃¹ non-axial terms through reversed-flux patches at the core-mantle boundary (r = 3,480 km).
dome_value: The dome claims the African cell decays faster 'because of its position relative to the pole axis.' But the dome's aetheric field is described as 'anchored at the north pole axis' — a fundamentally axisymmetric (azimuthally invariant) description. On the dome's AE-projected disc, both cells are at similar radial distances from the pole (r ≈ 12,787 km for 25°S). An axisymmetric field produces identical conditions at all points at the same radius. The dome's own geometry cannot distinguish between 30°E and 50°W at the same latitude — it treats them identically. To break this symmetry, the dome would need non-axisymmetric structure, which is never specified.
why_it_matters:
Three globe fingerprints in the differential decay claim:

(1) SYMMETRY BREAKING REQUIRES SPHERICAL HARMONICS: On a sphere, the geomagnetic field naturally decomposes into modes with azimuthal structure (m≠0 terms: g₂¹, h₂¹, etc.). These create longitude-dependent features like the SAA's two cells. On a flat disc with a pole-centered field, the natural decomposition uses Bessel functions Jₘ(kρ), which CAN have azimuthal variation — but the dome never specifies any m≠0 structure. Its 'aetheric field anchored at the pole axis' is a pure m=0 description. An m=0 field on a disc would produce a single annular anomaly, not two distinct cells at different longitudes.

(2) CIRCULAR PREDICTION CHAIN: WIN-005 fits an exponential to CHAOS-7 data (globe-model output). WIN-035 then extrapolates this curve forward and sets a threshold of 21,795 nT. When CHAOS-7's next update confirms the extrapolation, the dome claims a 'prediction confirmed.' But the prediction is: 'the globe model's output will continue to follow the trend we fitted to the globe model's previous output.' This is predicting that a globe model will continue to be self-consistent — not that dome physics produces a specific value.

(3) CMB RADIUS IMPLICIT DEPENDENCY: The factor-of-two differential between cell decay rates (African ~180 nT/yr vs Atlantic ~100 nT/yr) is explained by reversed-flux patches at the core-mantle boundary (r = 3,480 km = R_Earth − 2,891 km). This radius is an explicitly globe-derived quantity. The dome has no interior structure — no core, no mantle, no CMB. Without a CMB, there is no physical surface where reversed-flux patches can form, and therefore no mechanism for differential cell decay.
suggested_text: The dome claims the African cell decays faster 'because of its position relative to the pole axis,' but this explanation contains a fatal geometric contradiction. The dome's aetheric field is described as pole-anchored and axially symmetric — yet the two SAA cells sit at similar latitudes (similar distances from the pole) but different longitudes. An axisymmetric field cannot produce different decay rates at the same radial distance; breaking this symmetry requires the non-axisymmetric spherical harmonic terms (m≠0 Gauss coefficients) that arise naturally in globe geophysics but are never defined in the dome model. Furthermore, the 21,795 nT threshold 'predicted' for WIN-035 was fitted to CHAOS-7's spherical harmonic output and extrapolated forward — a circular prediction that the globe model will continue to be self-consistent.
